data.body:
Mechanism (verified from code, root cause of the crash localized): validate_to() in src/rebar/llm/structured.py:180-188 unwraps a top-level JSON array ONLY when it contains exactly ONE dict element (prior fix artsy-chain-hold/dash-lure-slag/slit-rubble-braid). The completion-verifier's Pass-3 'decide' step here returned a list with zero OR two-or-more dict elements, which is deliberately treated as ambiguous and raises 'structured output must be a JSON object, got list' -> fail-closed close block. So this is a MODEL-reliability defect (decide step emitting a non-single-object array) surfacing as a fail-closed gate crash.

To fix CORRECTLY needs the ACTUAL failing model output (zero-dict vs multi-dict list) — not captured here — plus a judgment call: (a) harden the decide step's prompt/schema to force a single verdict object, and/or (b) add a bounded-retry that feeds 'return a single JSON object' back, and/or (c) verdict-specific salvage (pick the element carrying a 'verdict' key) — the last would leak model-specifics into the generic validate_to, so likely belongs at the decide-step layer. NOT fixing speculatively without the captured output; needs a focused follow-up with a real reproduction (a billable verify-completion run on a weak-AC ticket).
